The Cloud Native paradigm let me experiment, fail fast, and try again without fear of lasting damage. In positive-sum collaborations, I saw how my algorithms could shape real-world outcomes, and how taking calculated risks created visible change for people and systems alike. This freedom to test ideas with relatively low cost revealed a new kind of agency, an immediacy and measurable connection to the meaning of work.My vision is to empower others to leverage cloud native technology with respect for the human element, security, and risk. We proved that microservices and containers aren't just limited to datacenters and the cloud but are also working great in the edge. Unfortunately there was no K8s for the edge like k3s at this time, so we used docker-compose.  Since 2019 I work as a platform engineer around Kubernetes and its ecosystem.

Led multidisciplinary teams of up to 100 members through year-long projects, including critical version upgrades and system stabilizations.Why I am excited to be an ambassador:I am deeply passionate about advancing the adoption and understanding of cloud-native technologies, particularly within the Korean technical community. Throughout my professional journey, I have been dedicated to open source advocacy, technical education, and community leadership, with a focus on bridging the gap between global cloud-native advancements and local practitioners. Notably, I have taken a leading role in organizing KCD Seoul and have served as an organizer for the Cloud Native Community Groups (Seoul), effectively acting as an ambassador for CNCF within Korea. Through these activities, I have empowered Korean developers and organizations to fully leverage CNCF projects by providing timely, accessible, and high-quality technical content, workshops, and mentorship programs. I am committed to reducing language and cultural barriers by localizing key resources, fostering inclusive community spaces, and encouraging active participation in global CNCF initiatives. As a CNCF Ambassador, I aim to be a trusted liaison, ensuring that the Korean community not only keeps pace with global trends but also contributes meaningfully to the evolution of the cloud-native ecosystem.
